name: TDD-SUBAGENT-BUG-33
title: tdd-step.py write_state is not atomic when read fails mid-write
description: tdd-step.py write_state (lines 100-110) opens fj for read, parses JSON, then writes back via tmp + os.replace. If the read or parse fails mid-execution, no error is propagated; the function will crash with an exception bubbling up. write_state is called from cmd_transition without try/except after the successful state validation, so an interleaving file system change could leave the user with a transition that printed 'spec -> spec-update' but failed to actually write. Wrap write_state in error handling and emit a clear ERROR before returning non-zero from the calling cmd_transition. Currently the user sees a stack trace.
